title: ficus nervosa subsp. minor
intro: Ficus nervosa subsp. minor, also known as the "Smaller Ficus" or "Indian Banyan," is a subspecies of the Ficus tree native to the tropical regions of India, particularly in the Western Ghats mountain range. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus nervosa subsp. minor include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular natural remedy in traditional Indian medicine. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
